question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Short answer - These look like multiple independent issues (not a single root cause). The Zurich Teams silence matches a known Direct‑Routing / media‑port firewall problem; the Basel printing problems match several different root causes seen in past tickets (network/VLAN reachability, queue state, and explicit permission denies)
context: SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. The old group was deactivated. 19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that: • Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups • Default printers per location are set via GPO • No parallel groups with identical function may exist Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group. As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default. 19.11.25 11:30 – Test Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership. gpupdate /force executed. User logged in again. Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”. 20.11.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization. Correction performed according to blueprint specification. 24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback Multiple users tested. Default printer remains correct. No further automatic switching. 24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 18 The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment. Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
